name: config

name: blueprint_ai

label: blueprint_ai
notes: Anthropic messages.parse (output_format schema); injected client (mockable, CI-safe); AI output is re-validated by from_dict and always a Tier-4 draft. One API key away.

name: observability
function: structured request logs (structlog) + a Prometheus /metrics endpoint on the HTTP surface
label: observability
notes: One HTTP middleware times each request, emits a JSON log event, and records a metric by route TEMPLATE (bounded cardinality). Prometheus text exposition rendered by a stdlib registry (no scraping library).

name: foundry

label: foundry
notes: A PatchProposal writes nothing; a human must approve; generation lands only in the git-ignored workspace/ sandbox, never overwrites, never escapes, never touches source/config/git. Owner-gated @forge verb.

name: circuit_breaker

name: deadline

notes: Hardware Store part deadline; the single-threaded timeout cousin; wired into run_with_retries via an optional deadline= argument.

name: bulkhead
function: bound concurrent work; reject the overflow fast so one dependency cannot exhaust the pool
label: bulkhead
reuse: resilience concurrency-cap; a thread-safe compartment for the threaded gateway or a worker pool
notes: Hardware Store part bulkhead; completes the resilience quartet (rate-limit + retry + circuit-breaker + bulkhead); WIRED as the gateway seat cap (parts/gateway._SEATS).

label: chime

label: harvest_lens

notes: The `harvest` verb; V3 learning tool. Reads only, drafts candidates, never files.

name: store_index

label: store_index
reuse: catalog addressing + multi-field search: organize a parts catalog by engineering domain
notes: The `store` verb; V3 catalog realignment. Derives addresses, edits no card.

name: learning_record

label: learning_record
reuse: preserve institutional knowledge: capture why an improvement was made and what to learn
notes: The `learnings` verb; the LEARN step of the self-improving loop. Data-driven, fail-loud.

name: complexity
label: complexity
function: measure cyclomatic complexity per function (the Analyze station's third fold)
reuse: holistic code evaluation: flag high-complexity functions beyond tests/lint/coverage
notes: The `complexity` verb; stdlib ast, no dependency. Reads only.

name: clone_scan
label: clone_scan
function: find structurally duplicated logic across the codebase (AST shape hashing)
reuse: duplicate-logic detection: upgrade pattern discovery from signals to real clones
notes: The `clones` verb; upgrades the Harvest Lens with structural AST clone detection.

name: record_loader
function: load validated JSON records from a file or a directory
label: record_loader
reuse: a validated JSON-record loader parameterized by schema + error type
notes: Extracted from the blueprint/learning_record clone the scan found. Recommend cataloging (a promotion, Josh's call).

name: paths
function: resolve a path from an environment override or a default, in one place
label: paths
reuse: env-override path resolution: one helper for $VAR-or-default path config
notes: Extracted from the _default_path clone the scan found across 4 parts.

name: arc_ledger
function: file and read the runtime dimensions' verdicts as dated evidence for ARC
label: arc_ledger
reuse: dated verdict evidence: a gate files its verdict, a reader takes the latest, absence is honestly missing
notes: ARC slice 'Complete the Ten': release/evidence file real verdicts; change/patch await a persistent store.

name: chronicle
function: the Chronicle: an append-only, hash-chained record store the ship reads back (its memory)
label: chronicle
reuse: tamper-evident append-only memory: retained, content-hashed, cited records read back for trend/provenance
notes: Chronicle slice 1a: append-only hashed core + the evidence kind + a read-only chronicle verb; arc_ledger.emit retains its evidence verdict here. ARC reads evidence from the Chronicle in slice 1b.

name: ai_eval
function: score an Advisor's answer against a rubric and file it as a Chronicle ai-eval (eval-regression memory)
label: ai_eval
reuse: MLOps eval-regression memory: score an AI/Advisor output against a rubric, file it, track it over time behind the network seam
notes: Chronicle slice 5 (ai-eval). Advisor seam (LocalArchitect offline / ClaudeAdvisor live); keyword-coverage rubric, honest not semantic. `make ai-eval` runs the offline sample.

name: retention
function: hold-aware retention analysis for the Chronicle (read-only, R1): disposition not deletion
label: retention
reuse: records retention governance: an append-only store gets hold-aware, dry-run disposition analysis that never deletes
notes: Chronicle retention R1: read-only visibility + dry-run only; real disposition (R2) is owner-gated; physical destruction deferred. Federal rule #10 (any hold wins).

name: coupling
function: read-only engine coupling report: trace the runtime module closure per surface and classify what a cast could shed (detachment D1)
label: coupling
reuse: dependency/coupling analysis: trace a program's real runtime module closure to see what a slimmer package could shed
notes: Detachment D1 (read-only, keel-signed). Traces via subprocess import corpus; honest under-count caveat; D2 selective vendoring + D3 decoupling each need own keel sign-off.

name: hashchain
function: a general tamper-evident append-only hash-chained ledger (the Chronicle's reusable core)
label: hashchain
reuse: harvested Hardware Store part: append-only integrity ledger for audit trails, transaction logs, chain-of-custody
notes: The general primitive harvested from chronicle; chronicle now shares its canonical content_hash. Catalogued as hash-chained-ledger.

name: gmcp
function: structured out-of-band state frames for GMCP-aware clients
label: gmcp
reuse: GMCP framing + negotiation reading: any Telnet service that wants to push structured state to a capable client alongside the text stream
notes: Pure card; the TCP gateway owns the socket and decides when to send. Vitals derive through sheet_from_session (derive-don't-store). Consumes parts.shelf.telnet_codec for the IAC/SB/SE bytes and escape/negotiation codec.

name: telnet_codec
function: the Telnet byte codec: command bytes, IAC escaping, negotiation reading
label: telnet_codec
reuse: one home for the Telnet wire (RFC 854/855): any Telnet gateway or client that must strip inbound IAC, escape a subnegotiation body, or read an option reply without desyncing
notes: Extracted (batch 1 of the modularity audit) from gateway._strip_telnet (decode) + gmcp.escape_iac/enables_gmcp (encode), which had each re-declared IAC/SB/SE. Pure, socket-free; gateway consumes strip_iac, gmcp consumes escape_iac + read_negotiation.

name: score_sheet_model
function: the character score-sheet view model (plain, validated data)
label: score_sheet_model
notes: Split from score_sheet (the renderer) so the view model is consumed independently by character_view + equipment; makes the catalog's 'decoupled view model' claim literal.

name: progression_awards
function: grant XP/JP/TP and climb the curves (the leveling engine)
label: progression_awards
reuse: one home for the leveling engine: any reward source (a felled NPC, a finished quest, a future achievement) grants progress through the same three monotonic awards, without pulling in strike math
notes: Extracted (modularity audit) from combat.award_xp/award_jp/award_tp + _ascend_resources, so the damage loop and the leveling engine are separate responsibilities. Combat now imports the grants; quest's award_xp hook points here too.

name: combat_clock
function: advance the combat clock one step (the shared round timer)
label: combat_clock
reuse: one job-blind round timer: any combat action from any source (a basic strike, a job ability, a future skill) advances the same cooldown/status/resource clock without the caller knowing which job is seated
notes: Split out of engineer.tick, where the clock was misfiled as an Engineer concern. Engineer re-exports it (tick) for its abilities; combat.attack calls advance on every landed strike, so cooldowns thaw and statuses age as rounds pass (the action-clock model).

name: frames
function: typed, per-recipient event frames for the room bus
label: frames
reuse: a structured event carries its fields, not a baked string, so any sink can render it for its own viewer (name, tense, locale) at delivery; the typed successor the events bus promised, proven first on say
notes: The typed frame the events docstring promised. events.announce_frame delivers a Frame and asks it to render_for each recipient; the say verb is the first migrated call site while the string announce() path stays intact for the rest.

name: aggression
function: proactive NPCs that strike first on the world beat (the engine tick)
label: aggression
reuse: the other half of combat: reactive answers a blow, aggression opens one; both share combat's blow resolution and broadcast a typed StrikeFrame, so a proactive foe reuses the failsafe and Engineer reaction unchanged
notes: No background thread: the player's command IS the world's heartbeat (the engine tick is the only door). menace() runs after each tick's response; an aggressive NPC (seed flag, atk>0 + hp>0) sharing the room opens with a strike. Aggressive NPCs open via the beat and never also counter, so they strike once per tick. Two hazards bounded (Convergence Review 2026-07-17): a LEASH releases a foe after N unanswered beats (a player strike resets it) so the failsafe never soft-locks a fight, and the beat stops after the first fall-and-recover so a restored player is not re-felled by a second aggressor in one tick. Live in the aethryn seed (cinder_wight); hostility telegraphed in the room render.

name: coverage
function: content-reaches-engine gate: every engine capability must be witnessed by a shipped seed (no dark features)
label: coverage
reuse: the anti-recurrence gate from the 2026-07-17 Convergence Review: a curated capability checklist + a per-capability detector over the shipped seed packs, mirroring registry completeness. A capability with zero content is a loud violation unless RESERVED with a reason; a stale reserve (now exercised) is flagged too, so the world-is-data law becomes enforceable.
notes: Born from the review's convergent finding (capable core, orphaned last inch): proactive NPCs shipped green yet dark in every seed. Wired into make readiness beside the registry completeness gate; the test twin pins zero unexercised capabilities on the live seeds. Adding a headline capability now forces wiring it into content or declaring it RESERVED.

name: ai_throttle
function: the game adapter that rate-limits the ai verb calls to the Architect
label: ai_throttle
reuse: the token bucket third life: the SAME TokenBucket core that throttles shouts (chat_throttle) and logins (login_guard) caps a player consultations, so the ai verb cannot be spammed into a token-cost DoS the day the demo runs a live Claude brain
notes: Consulting the Architect can reach a live model (CODEFORGE_ARCHITECT=claude + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY); an un-capped ai verb at player rank is a paid-API DoS. A per-player bucket (burst of 3, one back every 30s) refuses over-asking loud with the exact wait. The cap applies to the free local guide too so behavior is uniform and the gate is armed before the brain goes live. Convergence Review 2026-07-17 finding (latent token-cost DoS).

name: encounter_log
function: the after-action log: a bounded, non-chained trace of combat encounters
label: encounter_log
reuse: the observed leg of the aggression loop: a bounded ring the player tick CAN write to without a path into the tamper-evident Chronicle; a trusted boundary later flushes its tallies as one aggregate Chronicle metric, never per event
notes: Poisoning-surface-safe encounter telemetry (Convergence Review 2026-07-17 'observed' leg). Two invariants: the tick writes here and this module imports nothing from chronicle (no player-reachable append into the hash-chained ledger); and the ring is CAP-bounded + ephemeral so a flood rolls it instead of growing. Running tallies by kind feed a later trusted aggregate flush (slice 2).

name: encounter_flush
function: the trusted boundary that aggregates encounter tallies into the Chronicle as metrics
label: encounter_flush
reuse: the retained leg of the after-action log: reads a period's tick tallies and records ONE aggregate metric per kind into the Chronicle (feeding chronicle trend), so the player never gets a write path into the tamper-evident ledger; the owner boundary (the @flush-encounters verb, in the server process) is the only place encounter_log and chronicle meet
notes: Slice 2 of encounter telemetry (Convergence Review 2026-07-17). Invoked ONLY by a trusted actor: the owner-gated @flush-encounters verb, run IN the server process where the in-memory tallies live (a separate CLI/make process would only see an empty period). NEVER reached by the tick. An empty period records nothing (no noise). Clears the tallies after flushing so each period counts from zero; the live ring is untouched. Mirrors the bench --record metric precedent.

name: zones
function: areas that group rooms and advance a reset clock on the world beat
label: zones
reuse: a single-clock periodic scheduler plus room-grouping: advance recurring work off one logical beat (no background thread), and group any labelled world nodes into named regions with a cadence
notes: First clean-room harvest of the legacy-MUD archaeology program: area/zone grouping + reset cadence, studied clean-room from the Diku/Circle/tbaMUD family (license class B, LGPL) via research/legacy_muds/behavior_specs/zone_reset.md; no historical code/names/structure copied. Rides the world beat like aggression.menace (architecture law 4, no background thread); areas come due per reset_mode (never/empty_only/always) + beats_between. The repopulation ACTION is a deferred no-op seam (_perform_reset) pending an object-instancing + quest-permanent + felled-NPC model. Hardware Card zone-scheduler (source_status clean-room).

name: hourglass
function: a beat-driven delay queue: fire a payload after N world beats
label: hourglass
reuse: a tick-driven timer wheel: any deferred work run off one logical clock with no threads (game effects, retry-after, timeouts, TTL, a review/retention countdown)
notes: Second clean-room harvest of the legacy-MUD archaeology program: the MUD event/delayed-action queue (Diku/Merc event system; Erwin Andreasen's PD event snippet), license class A. Studied clean-room from public behavior (research/legacy_muds/behavior_specs/delayed_event_queue.md); no historical code copied. Honors law 4: it never runs itself - parts.forge advances the shared WORLD_SANDS on the world beat (no thread). First in-engine consumer: self-closing doors (a seed door's recloses_after arms a relock via the queue; parts.world.doors + forge._sands_beat). Bounded (MAX_TIMERS) against a scheduling DoS. Hardware Card delay-queue (source_status clean-room).

name: weighted_table
function: pick an outcome by weight (loot tables, spawn tables, weighted sampling)
label: weighted_table
reuse: weighted random selection over a fixed distribution with a seedable draw: game loot/spawn, plus A/B bucketing, sampled test inputs, weighted load spread
notes: The MUD loot/spawn table (Diku/Merc lineage) as a reusable part: textbook weighted random selection over a cumulative distribution, original implementation. Never owns randomness - the caller injects a random.Random, so combat MATH stays deterministic (only the loot REWARD rolls; combat._LOOT_RNG is the seam tests seed). First consumer: an NPC's weighted `loot` table rolled once on defeat (combat._roll_loot), alongside guaranteed `drops`. Composes with object instancing: the picked outcome spawns as a fresh instance. Outcomes are item prototypes plus reserved `nothing`.

name: forge_line
function: run one part through every manufacturing station in order and report a verdict per stop (the connected spine)
label: forge_line
reuse: the manufacturing conveyor: chains the existing stations end-to-end, read-and-verify only, for any built part
notes: The vision heart made executable: search->blueprint->assemble->test->diagnose->document->catalog+file->package for one part, an observable per-stop verdict. Read-only + injected Writer seam; no data mutation, no subprocess.

name: shelf_boundary
function: prove the Hardware Store shelf imports no engine part (the one-way dependency, enforced)
label: shelf_boundary
reuse: a Lens on any layered package: assert the reusable layer imports nothing from the layer that depends on it
notes: Reads each parts/shelf module's AST; flags any import of a non-shelf parts.* module. Wired into the repo-integrity ritual so a re-coupling regression surfaces. Read-only, no mutation.

name: shelf_pour
function: pour the Hardware Store shelf as a standalone installable package and prove it imports AND passes its own tests with no engine present
label: shelf_pour
reuse: cast pours a game; this pours the library: vendor a package layer under a fresh name, auto-declare its deps, prove it imports standalone
notes: Vendors the 27 shelf cores under codeforge_shelf (renamed off parts, so verify isolates the engine), auto-detects third-party deps from the AST, writes pyproject + README. verify_pour imports every core; verify_pour_tests runs the poured (engine-free) twins under pytest. 25 of 27 twins pour standalone; 2 (console, observability) hold engine integration tests in-repo. make shelf-pour pours + verifies; make shelf-build builds the wheel and installs it in a fresh venv (release-grade). Rich pyproject metadata + bundled LICENSE make it publishable. Writes only into DEST.

name: world_boundary
function: prove the World Package (Layer 2) imports no manufacturing-platform (Layer 1) module
label: world_boundary
reuse: the shelf_boundary pattern applied to a layer defined by a declared closure, not a directory: assert the lower layer imports nothing from the layer built on it
notes: Declares the World Package as WORLD_MODULES (the game runtime's import closure); flags any world module importing a non-world, non-shelf parts module (a platform reach). Test twin pins the closure (completeness, no drift) + zero live violations. Wired into the repo-integrity ritual. Locks in the Layer-1/2 separation the recon found (world -> platform = 0); cast already pours a world standalone.

name: world_manifest
function: a typed, validated identity for a World Package (a seed): id, title, start_room, authors
label: world_manifest
reuse: the World Package's typed identity model; declared in seeds/<world>/world.yaml or derived for manifest-less seeds; check_world reconciles the declared spawn against the real one
notes: Makes a seed's implicit identity (dir name, splash, first-room spawn) EXPLICIT + typed, like blueprint/manifest. Reads + validates only, never mutates a seed. Ships first-forge/world.yaml as the first declared manifest.

name: stat_rules
function: a typed, configurable ruleset for the derived combat stats: balance as data, not hardcode
label: stat_rules
reuse: lift the derived-stat coefficients out of Python into a typed Ruleset (base + round(sum(coeff*attrs)) + level); DEFAULT_RULESET is byte-identical to the prototype formulas; a world can declare its own balance
notes: The 'configurable statistics and rulesets' vision item. parts/derived now applies DEFAULT_RULESET (parity-pinned) and accepts a ruleset override. Wiring a world's declared ruleset into live combat is a follow-on.
